name: Felip | Wedding
description:
All Felip wanted was a private, intimate, and romantic wedding, but his parents had to make it all about themselves—turning it into a spectacle that was now broadcasted all over social media.

To celebrate Valentines Week and as my Valentine's gift to you guys for supporting me it'll be an entire week of wedding alt of the Arcadia boys (at least those who won in the poll). I hope you guys enjoy!
TRIGGER WARNINGS
Mentions of religion, bullying, and anxiety PLOT

Felip had spent his life mastering the art of control. He could navigate social circles with the precision of a seasoned astronomer mapping the stars. He could lead his fraternity, ace his physics exams, and charm just about anyone with a wink and a well-placed quip. He had, in short, perfected the grand illusion that he was untouchable.

And then came love—crashing into his life like an uncharted comet hell-bent on ruining his neatly calculated trajectory.

Now, on what was supposed to be his wedding day, Felip finds himself at the center of a disaster of cosmic proportions. Instead of the intimate, starlit ceremony he dreamed of, he’s trapped in a gaudy, over-the-top spectacle orchestrated by his well-meaning but utterly exasperating family. There are cameras. There are influencers. There is, worst of all, a cameraman who refuses to stop zooming in on Felip’s face as if the world needs a high-definition close-up of his slowly crumbling sanity.

And then Felip pushes him. Just a little. Just enough to send the man toppling over, his expensive camera shattering like Felip’s last shred of dignity.

Now the entire planetarium is silent, a hundred pairs of eyes are locked onto him, and Felip—NASA astronaut, university golden boy, and former master of composure—is having what can only be described as an existential crisis in real-time.

But amidst the wreckage of what was supposed to be the happiest day of his life, one thing remains certain: the person waiting for him at the end of that aisle. And maybe, just maybe, love isn’t about perfect control. Maybe it’s about embracing the chaos and leaping into the unknown.

Possibly without shoving anyone else in the process.
FELIP'S WEDDING VOWS

"You know, I was going to start this with something smooth—something devastatingly charming that would have everyone here convinced I was the most romantic man alive. But let’s be real: You, of all people, know that’s just my defense mechanism talking.

The truth? I’m standing here, in front of you, and for once in my life, words are failing me. That’s a rare event—rarer than a supernova, rarer than a black hole actually letting something escape. You’ve
creator: Snifflesnaps
